title: identity — tenancy hooks
description: Identity has NO tenancy hooks. The module is GLOBAL by design — there is no `tenant_id` on the `identities` table, and no `TenantProvisioned` / `TenantErased` action mutates the Identity substrate directly. Downstream cascade (e.g. TenantErased → UserErased → optional IdentityMarkedForErasure) is handled by the listener chain in listeners.json, not by tenancy hooks.
rationale: Tenancy hooks are for state that must materialise on tenant provisioning (default rows, seed data, per-tenant tables). Identity is a shared table with no per-tenant state — no provisioning is required when a new tenant appears.

Similarly, TenantErased does not directly touch identities. It cascades through Users (which ARE tenant-scoped): erasing a Tenant erases its Users, and if a User was the last inbound row for an Identity AND the compliance flag is set, `MaybeMarkIdentityForErasure` (a listener) picks up the signal.
Explicitly declaring `hooks: []` is a design invariant — any PR that adds a tenancy hook here is a mistake that mis-scopes the module.
